name: Frontend Lexer Number Suffix Validation
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-frontend/src/lexer.rs`, replace the stub at lines 380-385 (currently `self.diagnostics.push(...)` with warning about invalid suffix) with a proper validation function that checks the suffix against allowed integer/float suffixes (i8,i16,i32,i64,isize,u8,u16,u32,u64,usize,f32,f64). Emit a diagnostic with correct span when invalid suffix is found. Remove the `tracing::warn!` and implement `is_valid_number_suffix`. Add unit tests for valid/invalid suffixes.

name: Parser Unstubbing – All Missing Cases
scope_summary: Replace all `tracing::warn!("STUB: ...")` in `crates/glyim-frontend/src/parser/*.rs` with proper parsing logic. Specifically: `parse_token_tree` for macro repetition patterns (lines 220-250), `parse_type_arg_list` handling of `>>` (lines 520-540), `parse_where_clause` (lines 600-630), and `parse_visibility` for `pub(crate)` etc. Also implement missing `parse_label` (line 280-290) and `parse_loop_expr` (line 400-410). Remove all placeholder match arms that return `None` or `Err`.

tests.S02-T03: `where T: Clone + Send` parses as WhereClause
tests.S02-T04: `>>` in nested generic args parses as two `>` tokens

name: HIR Lowering – Exhaustive Pattern & Expr Coverage
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-hir/src/lower/lower_expr.rs`, replace the stub at lines 450-460 (`tracing::warn!("STUB: unhandled ExprKind")`) with exhaustive handling for `Expr::Closure`, `Expr::For`, `Expr::Range`, `Expr::Struct`, `Expr::Break`, and `Expr::Continue`. In `lower_pat.rs`, replace the stub at lines 320-330 (`tracing::warn!("STUB: unknown pattern kind")`) with full pattern lowering for `Pat::Or`, `Pat::Range`, `Pat::Struct` with `..`. Remove catch‑all `_ => Pat::Err` and `_ => Expr::Err`.

tests.S03-T01: `for i in 0..10 { }` lowers to valid HIR For
tests.S03-T03: `1..=5` range expression lowers to Expr::Range inclusive
tests.S03-T04: `Point { x, y }` struct expr lowers with field names

mocking: Use `glyim_test::phase::FrontendTester` to get AST, then assert HIR structure.

name: Type System – Auto Trait & AdtRepr Unstubbing
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-type/src/auto_trait.rs`, remove `tracing::warn!("STUB: no AdtRepr registered")` (line ~130) and implement proper ADT field iteration using the `adt_defs` map. Also implement `is_interior_mutable_adt` by checking if any field contains `UnsafeCell`. In `ty_ctx.rs`, implement `field_ty` using `adt_defs` first, fallback to `adt_reprs`. Remove all `unimplemented!()` in `ty_ctx_mut.rs` for methods like `register_adt`, `adt_def`, etc.

tests.S04-T02: `field_ty` returns correct type for ADT field index
tests.S04-T04: No `STUB` warnings emitted during type computation

name: Type Checker – Method Resolution & Pattern Exhaustion
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-typeck/src/check_expr.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: method resolution fallback")` (line ~600) with proper trait‑based method lookup using `TraitSolver`. Also replace the stub for `Expr::For` (line ~350) with full desugaring to `IntoIterator`. In `check_pat.rs`, remove the `tracing::warn!("STUB: unsupported pattern")` and implement pattern checking for `Pat::Or`, `Pat::Range`, `Pat::Struct`. In `tyconv.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: Cast PassMode")` (line ~200) with proper ABI handling.

tests.S05-T02: `for x in iter` desugars to `IntoIterator` and type checks
tests.S05-T03: Pattern `Some(x) | None` is checked with correct types

name: THIR to MIR Lowering – For‑loops, Closures, Pattern Matching
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-lower/src/lower_rvalue.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: for-loop lowering")` (line ~420) with full MIR lowering using `IntoIterator`. Also replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: closure lowering")` (line ~500) by generating an environment struct and `Aggregate` rvalue. In `lower.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: match lowering")` (line ~600) with decision tree lowering (SwitchInt for literals, discriminant for enums). In `lower_terminator.rs`, remove `tracing::warn!("STUB: drop glue scanning")` by implementing `Drop` terminator with correct drop function pointer.

tests.S06-T01: `for i in 0..10 { }` lowers to MIR loop with `next` calls
tests.S06-T02: Closure `|x| x+1` lowers to `Aggregate` with captured vars

name: MIR Interpreter – Missing BinOps and Projections
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-mir-interp/src/lib.rs`, replace all `tracing::warn!("STUB: ...")` with full implementations: `Len` for arrays/slices (line ~250), `Discriminant` for enums (line ~270), binary ops `BitAnd`, `Or`, `Xor`, `Shl`, `Shr`, `And`, `Or` (line ~300-350), and recursive projection for writes (lines 450-470 – `write_through_projections_with_locals`). Also implement `eval_unary_op` for `Not` on bools and `Neg` on ints.

tests.S07-T02: `match` on enum selects correct arm via discriminant
tests.S07-T03: `a & b` for integers computes bitwise AND

name: Bytecode Backend – Projections and Constants
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-codegen/src/lib.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: Dummy layout provider")` (line ~60) with a real layout provider using `glyim-layout`. Implement `emit_place_address` for all projections (Deref, Field, Index) – currently only works for `Deref` (line ~80-120). Implement `emit_operand` for `String`, `Fn`, and `ConstRef` constant kinds (line ~200-220). Remove `tracing::warn!("STUB: PassMode Cast")` (line ~400) and implement ABI‑aware argument passing (indirect, cast, homogeneous aggregate).

tests.S08-T01: Field projection emits correct offset using layout
tests.S08-T02: String constant emitted to string table
tests.S08-T03: Function constant emitted to function table
tests.S08-T04: Large struct passed indirectly via sret

name: LLVM Backend – All TyKind Mappings and Projections
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-codegen-llvm/src/types.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: unsupported float width")` (line ~40) with actual `f32`/`f64` mapping; also handle `TyKind::Dynamic`, `TyKind::Slice`, `TyKind::String`. In `lower.rs`, replace all `tracing::warn!("STUB: ...")` with proper LLVM lowering: `Deref` on non‑pointer (line ~300), `Field` on non‑aggregate using `adt_def` (line ~350), `Index` on non‑array (line ~400), `Len` with non‑integer count (line ~450), `Not` on float (line ~500), `Repeat` with non‑integer count (line ~550), `Assert` terminator (line ~700), and all `PassMode` variants (`Indirect`, `Cast`, `HomogeneousAggregate`, `Split`, `Ignore`) (line ~800-900).

tests.S09-T02: `dyn Trait` emits as `{ i8*, i8* }` (fat pointer)
tests.S09-T04: `&mut T` passed indirectly when size > 16 bytes

mocking: Compile MIR to LLVM IR and run `lli` to verify execution.

name: Pipeline – Monomorphization and Drop Glue
scope_summary: In `crates/glyim-pipeline/src/mono_cache.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: drop glue not found")` (line ~80) by implementing `generate_drop_glue` that walks the type fields and emits `Drop` terminators. In `pipeline_context.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("ADT id not found")` (line ~60) by actually building ADT definition from HIR if missing. In `mono.rs`, replace `tracing::warn!("STUB: drop glue scanning")` by implementing `scan_terminator` for `Drop` to collect types that need drop glue.

tests.S10-T01: Generic function `id<T>` monomorphized to `id<i32>` substitutes correctly
tests.S10-T02: Struct with `Drop` field generates drop glue that calls inner drop
tests.S10-T03: Enum drop glue switches on discriminant and drops active variant
tests.S10-T04: No `STUB` warnings emitted during pipeline execution

name: LSP – Reference Graph and Incremental Analysis

tests.S11-T02: Changing a file updates diagnostics incrementally
